title: RAG and Agent Workflows with LlamaIndex

modules.title: Why RAG Exists: Limitations of Base LLMs and When Retrieval Solves Them
modules.sub: Diagnosing where base LLMs fail and retrieval helps
modules.description: Learners examine the core limitations of static LLMs—knowledge cutoffs, hallucination, and context constraints—and map them to the problems RAG is designed to solve. They evaluate a set of real-world use cases to determine when retrieval is the right architectural choice versus when it is not. This diagnostic framing sets the decision-making foundation for the rest of the course.

modules.title: LlamaIndex Core: Documents, Nodes, Indexes, and Query Engines
modules.sub: Mastering LlamaIndex's fundamental data and query abstractions
modules.description: Learners work hands-on with LlamaIndex's core primitives—loading documents, chunking them into nodes, building indexes, and wiring up query engines. They trace how a user query flows through each layer of the framework to produce a grounded response. By the end, learners can construct a working end-to-end pipeline from raw documents to query results.

modules.title: Designing Retrieval Pipelines for Real-World Document Corpora
modules.sub: Architecting robust retrieval for messy, heterogeneous document collections
modules.description: Learners design retrieval pipelines that handle the realities of production corpora—mixed formats, inconsistent quality, and varying document lengths. They compare chunking strategies, embedding choices, and retrieval modes such as dense, sparse, and hybrid search, making deliberate trade-offs for their use case. The module culminates in a pipeline design decision log that justifies each architectural choice.

modules.title: Building Agents: Tool Use, Reasoning Loops, and Multi-Step Query Handling
modules.sub: Creating autonomous agents that plan, retrieve, and act across steps
modules.description: Learners build LlamaIndex agents that decompose complex queries into sub-tasks, select and invoke tools, and iterate through reasoning loops to arrive at a final answer. They configure tool definitions, manage agent memory, and observe how the reasoning loop behaves under different query types. This module shifts learners from passive retrieval to designing systems that can act autonomously on multi-step problems.

modules.title: Evaluating RAG Quality: Metrics, Failure Modes, and Iteration Strategies
modules.sub: Measuring retrieval and generation quality to drive systematic improvement
modules.description: Learners apply established RAG evaluation metrics—faithfulness, answer relevance, and context precision—to a test set and interpret the results to identify specific failure modes. They trace degraded outputs back to their root causes, whether in retrieval, chunking, or generation, and design targeted iteration strategies to address each. The module produces a reusable evaluation harness and a structured debugging checklist.

modules.title: Connecting LlamaIndex to External Data Sources and APIs
modules.sub: Extending RAG systems with live data sources and third-party integrations
modules.description: Learners connect LlamaIndex pipelines to external sources including databases, REST APIs, and cloud storage systems using LlamaHub loaders and custom connectors. They handle authentication, schema variability, and refresh cadence to keep indexes current with live data. By the end, learners have integrated at least one dynamic external source into a functioning retrieval pipeline.

modules.title: Scaling, Caching, and Cost Optimization in Production RAG Systems
modules.sub: Engineering RAG pipelines for throughput, reliability, and cost efficiency
modules.description: Learners analyze the cost and latency profile of a RAG pipeline and apply caching strategies, batching, and index optimization techniques to improve efficiency at scale. They make deliberate trade-offs between retrieval quality and infrastructure cost, documenting the impact of each change. The module prepares learners to operate RAG systems responsibly under real production constraints.

modules.title: Applying LlamaIndex to a Domain-Specific Professional Knowledge Base
modules.sub: Integrating course skills into a complete, domain-tailored RAG deployment
modules.description: Learners design and build a complete RAG-powered agent grounded in a domain-specific corpus of their choosing—such as legal documents, technical manuals, or financial reports. They apply the full stack of course skills: pipeline design, agent configuration, evaluation, and optimization, producing a system that meets defined quality benchmarks. The final deliverable is a documented, deployment-ready solution that demonstrates end-to-end professional competence.

synopsis: Retrieval-Augmented Generation has become the dominant architecture for grounding LLMs in organizational knowledge â and LlamaIndex is the leading framework for building these systems at scale. This course moves beyond conceptual overviews to give developers and technical professionals the design patterns and practical skills needed to build, evaluate, and maintain production RAG pipelines and autonomous agents.
rationale: RAG architectures are now the standard approach for enterprise AI applications that require accuracy and domain specificity, making LlamaIndex proficiency a critical skill for any professional building or evaluating AI systems.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to architect and deploy a RAG-powered agent that retrieves from a domain-specific corpus, handles multi-step queries, and meets measurable quality benchmarks for production use.
source_signals: Open source AI is the path forward (score: 5021)
